content.text:
I found the missing link вҖ” and it's the clever bit that ties your whole setup together.

## The complete CoH + modding ecosystem

You have **two entirely separate Wine worlds** on this machine, bridged by one symlink:

```
в”Ңв”Җ GAME WORLD (LaunchCat) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ  LaunchCat.app  вҶ’  Wine 9.0  вҶ’  prefix ~/.lcwine        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  install: ~/Library/Application Support/LaunchCat/coh/  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ    в”ңв”Җ bin/ assets/ (4.8G piggs) wine/ (797M)            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ    в”ңв”Җ accounts/ settings/ cache/ data/ logs/            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ    в””в”Җ launch.sh В· start-live.sh В· Homecoming.lnk        в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                    в–І
                    в”Ӯ  /Applications/coh  в”Җв”Җsymlinkв”Җв”Җв”җ
                    в”Ӯ  (0 B, no duplication)          в”Ӯ
в”Ңв”Җ MODDING WORLD (CoHModdingTool) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ  CoHModdingTool.app  ("CoH Launcher", Powermad)    в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ    = AppleScript applet that:                       в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ    1. seeds itself into ~/Library/App Support/CityModInstaller/ в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ    2. runs CoHModdingTool.exe under its OWN bundled в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ       Wine (2022) + OWN prefix (wineprefix/)        в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  config gamepath: z:\Applications\coh\  в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ    в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

`вҳ… Insight в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ`
- **The `/Applications/coh` symlink is the load-bearing glue.** The modding tool's config says `gamepath = z:\Applications\coh\`. In Wine, `Z:\` is mapped to the macOS root `/`, so `z:\Applications\coh\` resolves to `/Applications/coh/` вҶ’ (symlink) вҶ’ your real install. The symlink exists to give the Windows-side tool a **short, space-free** path вҖ” the real path (`.../Application Support/...`) has spaces that break Wine path handling. Delete that symlink and the modding tool loses the game.
- **Two Wine prefixes, deliberately isolated.** The game runs on modern Wine 9.0; the modding tool ships its own 2022 Wine + prefix so a game update can't break the tool and vice-versa. `CoHModdingTool.exe` is a **Mono/.NET** app (hence the `mono/eglib` lines in its log).
- **The applet is copy-on-first-run.** It seeds `CoHModdingTool.exe`, the wineprefix, x86/x64 dirs, and `modder.db` into `~/Library/Application Support/CityModInstaller/` only if missing вҖ” so the `/Applications` bundle stays pristine and your working copy + mod database live in App Support.
`в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ`

## Current status

| Thing | State |
|-------|-------|
| Modding tool seeded & launched | вң… Yes (00:12вҖ“00:14) вҖ” `CoHModdingTool.exe` ran under its Wine |
| Pointed at your game | вң… `gamepath z:\Applications\coh\` resolves correctly via symlink |
| `modder.db` tables | `config`, `installedMods`, `trackedFiles` вҖ” clean SQLite mod ledger |
| **Mods installed** | **0** вҖ” tracked files: 0. Tool is set up but nothing modded yet |
| Game `assets/mods/` drop point | Empty, and already on the launch line (`-assetpath assets\mods`) |

**Log health:** the CityModInstaller `launcher.log` is almost all **benign Wine noise** вҖ” `wineusb.sys` copy error 1812, `GetFileMUIPath` stubs, `ntlm_auth not found` are all harmless. The one line worth a note is the repeated `mono/eglib/gpath.c:115: assertion 'filename != NULL' failed` вҖ” that's the .NET runtime hitting a null path during startup. It doesn't appear to have stopped the tool (it launched twice cleanly), and `dontCheckforData: True` in the config suggests you already told it to skip data-path validation, which likely silences the practical impact.

## So the whole chain is: вң… **built, connected, and idle вҖ” ready to install its first mod.**

`modder.db` is the ledger that will track which mod owns which file in `assets/mods/`, so uninstalls are clean.

**Where do you want to go from here?** The obvious next step is to run CoHModdingTool, install a mod (it'll write into `assets/mods/` and record it in `modder.db`), and verify it loads in-game. I can drive that, dig deeper into any one piece, or chase down that Mono assertion if you'd rather have a spotless log first. Which?
